executive_summary: This session demonstrates strong room presence and a welcoming demeanor, with good distribution of attention. Focus on opening your posture between points and refining gesture size to enhance clarity and approachability. Overall, a solid foundation with clear areas for targeted refinement.

top_strengths.title: Alert room presence
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room. 00:15-00:30 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same quick room checks that make the lecture feel attentive.

top_strengths.title: Distributed room engagement
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room. 00:30-00:45 showed attention moving across more than one part of the room.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.

top_strengths.title: Welcoming visible tone
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 showed a more approachable visible facial tone. 00:15-00:30 showed a more approachable visible facial tone.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same relaxed facial reset that reads approachable on camera.

priority_actions.title: Open the stance between points
priority_actions.why_it_matters: A more open posture tends to read as more confident and easier to approach.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points. 00:15-00:30 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Let the elbows open slightly and release any folded or guarded arm positions between points.

low_confidence_watchlist.title: Tighten the peak size of gestures
low_confidence_watchlist.why_watch: The signal is visible, but it is not yet sustained enough to justify real corrective feedback.
low_confidence_watchlist.what_we_saw: 00:30-00:45 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required.
low_confidence_watchlist.what_to_monitor_next: Check whether gesture size matches the importance of the point instead of peaking on routine lines.

keep_doing: Keep the same quick room checks that make the lecture feel attentive.
Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.
Keep the same relaxed facial reset that reads approachable on camera.
watch_for: Check whether transitions keep the elbows and shoulders open instead of folding inward.
Check whether gesture size matches the importance of the point instead of peaking on routine lines.
confidence_notes: Tracking quality is stable enough for formative review, but the outputs remain heuristic proxies rather than direct teaching-quality measures.
